title: Medical Knowledge Test (20 MCQs)
description: A comprehensive test exam with 20 questions covering various medical topics for flow testing.

questions.text: Which organ is primarily responsible for detoxifying chemicals and metabolizing drugs?
questions.explanation: The liver is the body's primary site for detoxification and metabolism.

questions.text: What is the normal resting heart rate for a healthy adult?
questions.explanation: A normal resting heart rate for adults ranges from 60 to 100 beats per minute.

questions.text: Which vitamin is synthesized when the skin is exposed to sunlight?
questions.explanation: Vitamin D is produced in the skin in response to UV radiation from sunlight.

questions.text: What is the largest bone in the human body?
questions.explanation: The femur, or thigh bone, is the longest and strongest bone in the body.

questions.text: Which part of the brain controls balance and coordination?
questions.explanation: The cerebellum is responsible for coordinating voluntary movements and maintaining balance.

questions.text: What is the primary function of white blood cells?
questions.explanation: White blood cells (leukocytes) are part of the immune system and help fight infections.

questions.text: Which hormone regulates blood sugar levels?
questions.explanation: Insulin, produced by the pancreas, allows the body to use glucose for energy or store it for future use.

questions.text: What is the main gas inhaled by humans during respiration?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is essential for cellular respiration and is the primary gas absorbed from the air.

questions.text: Where in the human body is the pituitary gland located?
questions.explanation: The pituitary gland is located at the base of the brain, below the hypothalamus.

questions.text: Which blood type is known as the universal donor?
questions.explanation: O negative blood can be given to patients of any blood type in emergencies.

questions.text: How many chambers does the human heart have?
questions.explanation: The human heart consists of four chambers: two atria and two ventricles.

questions.text: What is the most common cause of a stroke?
questions.explanation: An ischemic stroke occurs when a blood clot blocks an artery to the brain.

questions.text: Which organ produces bile?
questions.explanation: The liver produces bile, which is then stored and concentrated in the gallbladder.

questions.text: What is the medical term for high blood pressure?
questions.explanation: Hypertension is the clinical term for consistently high blood pressure.

questions.text: Which system is responsible for the transport of nutrients and gases throughout the body?
questions.explanation: The circulatory system (cardiovascular system) transports blood containing nutrients and oxygen.

questions.text: What is the approximate total volume of blood in an average adult?
questions.explanation: An average adult has about 4.5 to 5.5 liters of blood.

questions.text: Which nutrient is the primary source of energy for the body?
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates are the body's main source of fuel, especially for the brain and muscles.

questions.text: What is the function of the alveoli in the lungs?
questions.explanation: Alveoli are tiny air sacs where oxygen enters the blood and carbon dioxide leaves it.

questions.text: Which enzyme in saliva starts the digestion of starch?
questions.explanation: Salivary amylase breaks down complex carbohydrates into simpler sugars.

questions.text: What is the outer layer of the skin called?
questions.explanation: The epidermis is the outermost layer of the skin, providing a waterproof barrier.
